questions.question: What are the three parts of a standard for loop header in Java?
How many times does the initialization section of a for loop execute?
options: Once at the very start of the loop
questions.answer: Once at the very start of the loop

questions.question: When is the condition in a for loop checked?
options: Before each iteration, including the first
questions.answer: Before each iteration, including the first

questions.question: What is the output of: for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) { System.out.print(i + " "); }
How many iterations does the loop execute: for (int i = 0; i <= 5; i++)?
How many iterations does the loop execute: for (int i = 1; i < 5; i++)?
What is the correct order of execution in a standard for loop?
What does for (;;) { } represent in Java?
Which of the following is the correct syntax for an enhanced for loop?

What does the enhanced for loop read as in plain English?

for (String name : names)
options: for name at index of names
for String equals name in names

questions.question: What is a key limitation of the enhanced for loop compared to the standard for loop?
options: It does not provide access to the index
It can only be used with Strings
It runs slower than a standard for loop
questions.answer: It does not provide access to the index

questions.question: What happens when you modify the loop variable inside an enhanced for loop iterating over an int array?
options: The original array element is modified
The original array element is NOT modified
questions.answer: The original array element is NOT modified

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid for loop that counts down from 10 to 1?
Which of the following correctly prints even numbers from 2 to 20?

What does the semicolon at the end of a for loop header indicate?

for (int i = 0; i < 5; i++) ;
options: The loop has a syntax error
The loop has an empty body
questions.answer: The loop has an empty body

questions.question: Which loop type should you use when you need to modify array elements during iteration?
What is the valid way to loop through characters of a String using an enhanced for loop?
Which of the following for loops will print the uppercase letters A through Z?
What is an off-by-one error?
options: A loop that skips every other element
A loop that iterates one time too many or too few
A loop that starts at index 1 instead of 0
A loop that uses the wrong data type
questions.answer: A loop that iterates one time too many or too few

questions.question: Which statement about the enhanced for loop is TRUE?
options: It can iterate backward through an array
It automatically provides an index variable
It works with arrays and collections
It allows you to remove elements during iteration
questions.answer: It works with arrays and collections
